channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Spain’s prime minister visits China to strengthen ties with Trump's top tariff target
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is visiting China on Friday, his third trip to the country in two years as his government seeks to boost investment from the Asian giant amid global economic uncertainty caused by a chaotic U.S. tariff policy.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ksenia Karelina, a dual US-Russian national released by Moscow in prisoner swap, arrives in US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Russian-American woman freed by Moscow arrived back in the United States late Thursday as part of a prisoner swap completed as the two countries aim to repair ties. A plane carrying Ksenia Karelina landed around 11 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea establishes diplomatic relations with Syria, a longtime friend of rival North Korea
item.description: South Korea has established diplomatic relations with Syria’s new Islamist government months after longtime leader Bashar Assad was ousted.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel releases Palestinian who took part in attack at 13 and developed mental illness in prison
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel on Thursday released a Palestinian who took part in an attack when he was 13 and developed schizophrenia in prison as requests for early release were denied. Israel says Ahmad Manasra is a terrorist who tried to kill Jews his own age.
item.dc:creator: MAHMOUD ILLEAN and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yellen: Trump 'has taken a wrecking ball' to economy with trade war
item.description: Former Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen, who served under former President Biden, said President Trump “has taken a wrecking ball” to the economy.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Israel releases 10 Palestinians detained from Gaza. They say they suffered abuse
item.description: Israel has freed 10 Palestinians detained from Gaza, the latest in periodic releases from the thousands of people troops have seized during the military campaign in the territory.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taliban morality enforcers arrest men for having the wrong hairstyle or skipping mosque, UN says
item.description: The Taliban morality police in Afghanistan have detained men and their barbers over hairstyles and others for missing prayers at mosques during the holy month of Ramadan, a U.N. report said Thursday, six months after laws regulating people’s conduct came into effect.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis meets privately with King Charles and Queen Camilla during his Vatican convalescence
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis met privately with King Charles III and Queen Camilla on Wednesday at the Vatican during the royal couple's four-day state visit to Italy and on the occasion of their 20th wedding anniversary, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, DANICA KIRKA and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Officials scramble to ID victims of Dominican nightclub roof collapse
item.description: Crews at the scene were still looking for victims and potential survivors, although no one has been found alive since Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strike on Gaza apartment building kills at least 23, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli aircraft struck a residential block in war-ravaged northern Gaza on Wednesday, killing at least 23 people, health officials said, as the renewed fighting in the devastated Palestinian enclave showed no signs of slowing.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama and US lock in new security pact for Canal as China tensions simmer
item.description: Panama and the U.S. signed a new security pact on the canal, boosting defense ties as tensions with China over Latin American influence continue to rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump hits back with a 125% tariff in escalating trade war with China
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — President Donald Trump raised the tariff on Chinese imports to 125% on Wednesday, hours after China boosted the duty on American goods to 84% in an escalating battle that threatens to disrupt trade between the world's two largest economies.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee as Trump's ambassador to Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate voted Wednesday to confirm former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee as the Trump administration’s ambassador to Israel, two days after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's White House visit.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: At least 124 dead after roof collapse at Dominican club, officials scramble to ID victims
item.description: Rescue crews from Puerto Rico and Israel arrived Wednesday to help with the search. Officials said crews used sonar to detect what could be one person still breathing under the rubble.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nicaragua’s crackdown on the Catholic Church is worsening, exiles and human rights advocates say
item.description: Preparations for popular, often daylong Lent and Holy Week processions are underway across Latin America — but not in Nicaragua. They’ve been largely banned for a second year, one of many concerns for the faithful in a country that human rights advocates, exiled priests and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's president insists Tehran does not seek a nuclear bomb and dangles US business opportunities
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran's president again pledged Wednesday that his nation is “not after a nuclear bomb” ahead of talks between Tehran and the United States, going as far as dangling the prospect of direct American investment in the Islamic Republic if the countries can reach a deal.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida men charged in 'truly heinous' global child porn ring involving over 1M files
item.description: The Florida Department of Law Enforcement arrest seven people in Florida for an international child pornography ring involving over one million files purchased through social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson pecked by ostrich
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson had a fowl encounter with an ostrich during a sight-seeing trip in Texas, with video showing him getting nipped by the bird while his son laughed in his lap.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: China threatens countermeasures after Trump threatens additional 50% tariff
item.description: China threatened Tuesday to take countermeasures against the United States "to safeguard its own rights and interests” in response to President Donald Trump’s threat of an additional 50% tariff on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: South Korea says 10 North Korean soldiers crossed military demarcation line, warning shots fired
item.description: South Korea's military said troops aired warning broadcasts and fired warning shots after approximately 10 North Korean soldiers crossed the military demarcation line in the demilitarized zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, Netanyahu to meet at White House as Israel seeks tariff relief, discussions on Iran, Gaza hostages
item.description: President Trump and Israeli PM Netanyahu set for White House meeting to discuss ending U.S. tariffs on Israel, the war in Gaza, the release of hostages and Iran's nuclear threat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian air strikes kill 1 in Kyiv as Zelenskyy demands more pressure on Putin
item.description: One person was killed in Russian air strikes on Kyiv on Sunday while Kryvyi Rih mourns 19 victims of Friday's attack, which local authorities say also destroyed dozens of homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope appears in St. Peter's Square for first time in weeks
item.description: Pope Francis greeted crowds in St. Peter's Square on Sunday, his first public appearance since being discharged from the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN global comms arm under fire for anti-Israel bias as critics call for reforms
item.description: The U.N.'s Department of Global Communications is under fire for a one-sided view of the Israel-Palestinian conflict, with critics saying it often sides with Palestinians at the expense of other conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China slaps a 34% tax on all US imports in retaliation for Trump's tariffs
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tax on all U.S. imports next week, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new tariffs that delivered the strongest response yet from Beijing to the American leader's trade war.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, ELAINE KURTENBACH and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, NewsNation
